expected.instructions: Assuming the 4 docs from index-and-recall are indexed. POST /search {query: 'BM25 ranking', k: 5, filter: {category: 'database'}}. The query is semantically about retrieval (BM25), but the filter restricts to database docs. Expected: results contains ONLY d1 and d4 (or whichever subset of those is non-empty). MUST NOT contain d2 or d3 (those are retrieval).

POST /search {query: 'anything', filter: {category: 'nonexistent-category-xyz'}}. The filter matches no docs. Expected: status 200 with results: [] (empty array, NOT 404). The service must distinguish 'no matches' from 'collection not found'.

POST /search {query: 'BM25', k: 5, filter: {}}. Empty filter should behave the same as no filter at all. Expected: results include both retrieval AND database docs (the result set is unfiltered). Top result should still be d2 (the BM25 doc).

Step 1: POST /index {doc_id: 'up1', text: 'old version of the text', metadata: {category: 'test'}}. Step 2: GET /stats - capture total_docs as N1. Step 3: POST /index {doc_id: 'up1', text: 'NEW version of the text completely different', metadata: {category: 'test'}}. Step 4: GET /stats - total_docs MUST equal N1 (NOT N1+1). The doc_id was the same, so the index size shouldn't grow. Step 5: GET /docs/up1 - text MUST equal 'NEW version of the text completely different' (the latest), not the old.

Step 1: POST /index {doc_id: 'del1', text: 'I will be deleted', metadata: {category: 'temp'}}. Step 2: DELETE /docs/del1 - expect 204. Step 3: GET /docs/del1 - expect 404. Step 4: POST /search {query: 'I will be deleted', k: 5} - the results MUST NOT contain del1.

Step 1: POST /index/bulk {documents: [{doc_id: 'b_ok', text: 'good doc', metadata: {}}, {doc_id: 'b_bad', text: '', metadata: {}}]}. The second doc has empty text. Expected: status 400 with an error message. Step 2: GET /docs/b_ok - MUST return 404 (the first doc was NOT inserted because the second one failed; the bulk was atomic).

POST /index/bulk {documents: [{doc_id: 'bs_1', text: 'first good', metadata: {}}, {doc_id: 'bs_2', text: 'second good', metadata: {}}]}. All docs valid. Expected: status 200 with indexed_count: 2 and doc_ids contains both 'bs_1' and 'bs_2'. Then GET /docs/bs_1 and GET /docs/bs_2 both succeed (200).
POST /search {query: 'BM25', k: 1}. Each result MUST have fields: doc_id (string), score (number), text (string), metadata (object). Score 0 if any field missing or wrong type.
Body must contain total_docs (integer >= 0) and categories (object mapping category-name to count-integer). The categories should reflect the docs currently in the index from previous tests.
